title: Funky Uruguayan Cranberry Empanadas
description: Post-workout snack from Uruguay featuring cranberry and flamb1 flambe. Great for kosher-friendly diets.

ingredients.name: fresh or frozen cranberries
ingredients.notes: fresh for better flavor, frozen for longer shelf life

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, kosher salt, and granulated sugar.
Gradually add unsalted butter, using a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until crumbly.
Beat in large egg until the dough comes together, then turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 2-3 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide dough into 4 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.

In a small bowl, mix together cranberry sauce, chopped cranberries, and dried orange zest. Place 1 tablespoon of this mixture onto one half of each dough circle, leaving a 1/4 inch border around the edges.
Brush edges with water and fold dough in half to enclose filling, pressing edges to seal. Press the edges with a fork to crimp and decorate.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter over medium-high heat. When melted, add a few empanadas and sear until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Transfer empanadas to a baking sheet, seam-side down, and bake for 12-15 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let empanadas cool for 2-3 minutes on a wire rack before dusting with confectioner's sugar.
If desired, serve with a side of orange juice for an extra burst of citrus flavor.
